message:
fix(install): use anchored regex for more robust version string extraction

Changed sed version-extraction pattern from '.*([0-9]+...)' to
'[^0-9]*([0-9]+...)' so the regex anchors on the first numeric
sequence rather than greedily consuming the entire prefix, preventing
misparse when the --version output contains unexpected leading text.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -519,7 +519,7 @@ check_installed_version() {
   fi
 
   local installed_version
-  installed_version=$("$DEST/$BINARY_NAME" --version 2>/dev/null | head -1 | sed -E 's/.*([0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+).*/\1/')
+  installed_version=$("$DEST/$BINARY_NAME" --version 2>/dev/null | head -1 | sed -E 's/[^0-9]*([0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+).*/\1/')
 
   if [ -z "$installed_version" ]; then
     return 1
